comment_text.sn9.10:1.2: The verb saṅkasāyati is a synonym of allīyati, to stick or adhere to (Kd 3:1.2.3).
comment_text.sn9.10:2.1: At AN 4.191:1.7, dhammapada refers to all nine categories of the teaching, and I think it has the same sense here.
comment_text.sn9.10:3.1: The sense of this unusual five-line verse is not entirely clear to me, and I suspect it is corrupt.

root_text.sn9.10:1.1: Ekaṁ samayaṁ aññataro bhikkhu kosalesu viharati aññatarasmiṁ vanasaṇḍe.
root_text.sn9.10:1.2: Tena kho pana samayena so bhikkhu yaṁ sudaṁ pubbe ativelaṁ sajjhāyabahulo viharati so aparena samayena appossukko tuṇhībhūto saṅkasāyati.
root_text.sn9.10:1.3: Atha kho yā tasmiṁ vanasaṇḍe adhivatthā devatā tassa bhikkhuno dhammaṁ asuṇantī yena so bhikkhu tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā taṁ bhikkhuṁ gāthāya ajjhabhāsi:
root_text.sn9.10:3.4: Yaṁ kiñci diṭṭhaṁva sutaṁ mutaṁ vā;

translation_text.sn9.10:1.1: At one time one of the mendicants was staying in the land of the Kosalans in a certain forest grove.
translation_text.sn9.10:1.2: Now at that time that mendicant had previously been spending too much time rehearsing. But some time later they adhered to passivity and silence.
translation_text.sn9.10:1.3: Not hearing the teaching, the deity haunting that forest approached that mendicant, and addressed them in verse:
translation_text.sn9.10:2.1: “Mendicant, why don’t you recite <j>passages of the teaching,
translation_text.sn9.10:2.3: Hearing the teaching, one garners confidence;
translation_text.sn9.10:2.4: and in this very life, one garners praise.”
translation_text.sn9.10:3.1: “I used to be zealous for <j>passages of the teaching,
translation_text.sn9.10:3.4: whatever was seen, heard, and thought,
translation_text.sn9.10:3.5: this, good people say, <j>is the laying down by complete understanding.”
